mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It provides automated provisioning, scaling, and management of Kubernetes clusters, allowing users to focus on their applications rather than infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's cloud services and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This command installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This installs the OCI CLI using a bash script.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud. Specify the desired version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePools": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual IDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePools": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region>

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual values.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/oci-cloud-controller-manager.yaml

kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

```

> This deploys the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.
> Replace <your_user_id> with your actual user ID.
commands: kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force --wait-for-state DELETED

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Remove OCI CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the OCI CLI from your local machine.

```bash

rm -rf ~/.oci

```

> This removes the OCI CLI configuration and installation.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your workloads and configurations. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.30.2

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.
> Verify that the nodes are updated and in 'Ready' status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This typically occurs when the kubeconfig file is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command to ensure it is configured properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this action'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user does not have the necessary RBAC permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the user has the cluster-admin role or appropriate permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the node is not able to communicate with the control plane.

**Fix:**
Check the node's network configuration and ensure it can reach the control plane.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer not provisioning
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the subnets are not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify that the load balancer subnets are correctly set up in the OCI console.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features such as PodSecurity admission and improved scheduling capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs in favor of newer versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure that your workloads are compatible with the new API versions before upgrading.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePools": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'
kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
